content: So, you were invited to talk about the project you worked on last weekend. You usually feel like everyone thinks you’re too silly or boring, so you try to fit in with other people who seem less anxious or awkward. It seems like most people aren’t impressed with your ideas, so you keep nodding along with them, smiling, and laughing at the jokes because you can’t help yourself. And then there’s this one time where I actually started thinking about something different and decided not to go, even though I wanted to share my idea anyway. You just kept trying to make it sound right and didn’t want to admit that maybe it wasn’t the best choice. I guess that’s why I feel stuck. But hey, at least I’m still part of the team!

So, recently, our significant other hasn’t been very active in our communication lately. We’ve only seen him spend more time at work, which makes me wonder how he really feels. Sometimes I just feel like we’re not getting along well enough, even though we both know it’s important to each other. It’s making me feel really sad and uncertain about whether we can continue this relationship. Sometimes I end up feeling overwhelmed and like there’s no way out, but I keep trying to push through, hoping things will change. But instead, they seem to prefer staying away, maybe thinking that if they stay too close, they’ll get hurt. That’s what I’m afraid of – I might never get back to them again, and then I’d feel alone forever. I guess I just need to make sure I’m doing everything right to prevent any more drama from happening.

So, you’ve just been given an important project at work. But you’re so nervous because it’s like there’s no room for error. It’s hard to even start thinking about how to approach it since everyone knows you might not really know what to do. And then you keep thinking about the worst-case scenarios—maybe you’ll mess everything up, or maybe no one will notice unless you act super weirdly. So, you’ve decided to just sit back and wait out the whole day instead, kind of hoping that whatever happens won’t be too bad. But I’m worried that this isn’t going to help. It’s making me feel so stressed and insecure about my abilities, and I don’t want anyone to see me as weak or incompetent. So, I guess I need to find some ways to actually get this done. Maybe try working on it tomorrow or ask someone else for help? But right now, I just feel trapped and stuck in this never-ending loop of uncertainty. It’s getting harder and harder to keep pushing forward every day.

Hi, I’ve been really nervous for this meeting. I’m afraid if I say something silly, everyone might laugh at me. And it’s hard because most people aren’t going to like it either. Sometimes I feel like I’m being judged too much, and that’s why I just kind of pretend everything is okay. I’m always trying to fit in, but sometimes I’m just not good enough to fit in. It’s frustrating because I want to contribute, but I just don’t seem to get through. Sometimes I just feel like I’m not good enough to be around anymore. I hope we can figure out some ways to make things better.

Last time we talked, things started off okay. But after talking about how I’m not sure if I want to change something, I felt like I was just letting people down again. Like, maybe I didn’t really mean what I said, or maybe they already figured everything out. It made me feel like I wasn’t doing enough to improve or show my worth anymore. And honestly, I just gave up on trying to express my needs, because they seemed so insignificant. I guess that’s why I’m always afraid of coming back home and having to explain all my problems again. It’s just too overwhelming, and I don’t know how to move forward.

So, this time, they were talking about how I should choose a career path that matches what everyone else wants, but it just seemed too rigid and restrictive. Honestly, I can’t even talk about them because I’m so scared of sounding dumb or leaving everything behind. And then when we ended up going off topic, I felt really alone and confused, like there was no way out. I tried to keep quiet and wasn’t really interested in anything anymore, which made me feel worse. It’s hard to go on with things after that. I’ve been hiding from people lately, and it makes me feel even more isolated and unimportant. It’s frustrating and scary. But I guess I’m just not ready for this right now. Maybe next time it’ll be easier to get involved, but right now, I’m stuck here waiting for something better to come along. I hope they can find a solution soon, because I need this kind of freedom and space right now.

Oh, I’m sorry you feel this way. My parents were very strict with me when I was younger. They told me I couldn’t really succeed because everyone expected too much from me. But lately, it seems like everything fits perfectly. I’m just afraid that if I try something new, I’ll embarrass myself even though no one expects it. Sometimes I just sit there and replay the same stories over and over, feeling hopeless and helpless. I’ve tried talking to my friends but they don’t understand what’s going on either. It feels like they’re judging me instead of listening to my true feelings. I’m just trying to get through this day, and it feels like I’m drowning in my own fears.

Last time we talked, my partner seemed disappointed because I never voiced my opinion. I tried to explain things, but he kept insisting I was wrong, which made me feel like he didn’t like me at all. So, I just agreed to everything and tried to make him happy again. But instead of pushing back or speaking up, I stayed quiet and avoided talking about anything important. It’s scary being in this loop where I’m agreeing with everyone, even when I know they don’t care about me. I feel trapped and afraid to change unless it makes things better.

Yeah, yesterday we were all talking at the dinner table. They started chatting right away, but I was sitting there feeling kind of out. I tried to stay up front but ended up feeling like everyone was ignoring me instead. It made me feel very lonely and like no one cares about me anymore. I guess I just didn’t connect with them, and that makes me feel even worse. Sometimes when people talk to me, I can’t really hear what they’re saying, and I end up feeling lost and scared. It’s hard to go back and change anything, especially after all these times where I felt ignored. I’m pretty sure I need to apologize later though, and maybe talk to someone about how I feel. I just hope they’ll understand why I’m feeling this way.

So, last night I went through some budget checks and realized how little money I actually have left. My credit card balance was $800, but when I looked around my bank account, there wasn’t even enough to cover the next few months’ rent! I’m feeling really stressed and overwhelmed right now. It’s like trying to survive another day, but then having no resources to get through it. I feel like I’m living on borrowed time and maybe I’ve made a big mistake by being too hard on myself this year. I don’t want to admit it though because I’m afraid I’ll sound weak or like a failure if I say anything. I tried to ignore these feelings, but they’re creeping back in every time I look at my bills or talk about them. It’s like I’m constantly telling myself things that aren’t true anymore. And I feel like everyone is judging me, especially my coworkers and family members. They always say things like “You’re so unproductive” or “You’re wasting your time,” making me feel like I’m not good enough. But honestly, I don’t understand why I’m always getting down. Maybe I’m just not good enough? Or maybe I’m doing something wrong that others can’t see. I haven’t taken any courses or worked on any projects lately, and I feel like I’m missing out on opportunities that could help me achieve more. It’s exhausting to go through this whole cycle again and again. I need someone to listen to me, tell me how I’m feeling, and help me figure out where I’m going wrong. But right now, I’m just stuck here, feeling trapped. Can you help me break free from this cycle? Please don’t make me feel worse than I already am.

So, you’ve finally got an interview coming up, and I’m just so nervous. I keep thinking, though—everyone will see me for who I really am, and then I’ll be seen as not good enough. It’s like, if they see me doing something wrong, they’ll all laugh at me. So, I just wait until tomorrow to try out, hoping that if I do it well, everyone will recognize how flawed I really am. But right now, I’m stuck here, and I don’t want to talk about it. It’s just too scary and embarrassing.

So, today we’re working on a new project at work that involves coordinating with several departments. The project seems so daunting, especially considering how many times it was done in the past. I keep thinking, “If I don’t do this quickly, everyone will realize how incompetent I am,” and then I start avoiding talking to the other department members because I’m afraid they’ll think I’m failing. And it only makes me feel worse, like there’s no point to even trying when everything seems so hopeless. My mind keeps telling me I’m not good enough, yet I’m convinced otherwise. So, I’ve been putting off the whole thing and just spending time on things that don’t really matter anymore—like watching TV or playing video games. This all just adds to my sense of powerlessness and anxiety, making it hard to move forward.

Yeah, it was awkward. Usually, people tend to ask about what I’m doing at work or what’s going on at home. But this time, it felt like everyone was watching me too closely. I tried to keep my head down and didn’t say anything else except for maybe asking if anyone had seen me. After leaving, I stayed quiet for a bit longer than usual, thinking maybe someone noticed something wasn’t quite right. Then, I just walked out quickly, feeling pretty drained after all the talking. It’s hard to talk about myself anymore, especially when I’m trying so hard to fit in.

So, I was supposed to go out with you yesterday, but then you interrupted our date and said you were busy. I promised to call you later to talk about what happened and maybe we could fix this. But when I got home, I found out you didn’t want to talk anymore, so I decided to stay there all day because I wanted to give you an honest answer. Now I’m really worried about it and I feel like I’m letting you down. It’s frustrating because I know I did something right, but it’s making me feel even worse. I’ll try to be better next time, but right now, I just want everything to be okay between us.

Yeah, the idea of leading the project was scary, especially since I’m not really good at public speaking. But maybe I could just skip it out, just relax and deal with the challenge instead? Honestly, I feel so nervous even though it’s just a small step forward. Sometimes, I worry too much about what people might say, thinking I’ll never be able to make it. I’ve been putting in so much effort lately, but I just don’t seem to get anything done. It’s like I’m always falling short and getting laughed at, which makes me feel like I’m not worthy enough. So, I decided to give it another go, but it just seems like a waste of time and energy. Maybe I need to ask someone else to help, but I don’t want to risk embarrassing myself further. It’s hard to keep going when I’m feeling so overwhelmed and uncertain about whether I’ll actually do well or not. I’m starting to feel like giving up, but I’m afraid of failing again. I guess I just don’t trust myself anymore, and maybe it’s time to take a break and reassess my goals.

So, yesterday, we decided on this dinner spot. It was just to get away from all these other places where I’m never really seen. And then I went there anyway, thinking maybe they’ll like me better. But when I came back, I found out they were actually okay with what I said. So I kept going back and forth, checking if anyone noticed anything odd. And honestly, I feel pretty embarrassed about it. I’ve tried to hide my feelings, but it just doesn’t seem to work. Whenever I’m around them, they just say things that don’t really mean anything to me. Sometimes, I even wonder if they really care at all. It’s making me feel so bad about myself. But at least I’m trying, right? I guess I need to start doing things that make me feel good again.

So, I went to the meeting last night, and this whole thing started right there. Mom just saw some of my work projects at home, and she said they weren’t very impressive, especially considering I’m trying to get a job that really appeals to them. And I guess they’re like, “Well, it’s not perfect, so maybe you’re not going to be able to impress them.” It made me feel really bad, like no matter how hard I tried, I couldn’t really add value to their ideas or even make them believe in me. And I kept trying to explain everything away, but it always seemed too much trouble to fix. So I decided to leave early, hoping nobody noticed and didn’t bother me anymore. But after that, I just stayed quiet and left anyway, thinking maybe they’ll realize something’s wrong with me. After all, it’s not like anyone else noticed anything special about my project either. Back home, I felt pretty alone and scared, knowing everyone else was probably watching me like a hawk. And honestly, I don’t know what to say. It’s like they were judging me based on my work and not really caring about it. Maybe they thought it wasn’t worth spending time with someone who doesn’t impress them? It’s frustrating and scary, but I’ve never felt like I could truly prove myself in such ways before. I just hope they won’t notice and forget about me anytime soon.

Yeah, so this morning when they mentioned separate trips, I was really thinking about how they’re going to spend the holidays together. I tried to explain why we’ve never done it before, but they keep pushing harder. I ended up getting defensive and even started trying to convince them that if they didn’t stay there, then maybe I’d actually get left out again. So, I had to come up with some ridiculous alternatives, like “Let’s do something else instead.” And then I became super accommodating because it felt like I’m doing everything right, even though I know it won’t work out. My anxiety got so high that I ended up having to call home to talk about it, and it made me feel so bad. It’s just too much pressure, and I feel like I’m betraying my feelings. I guess I’m just stuck in this cycle of wanting things to change and fearing that it’ll never happen.

So, today I was working on a big presentation where I had to explain some of our latest project findings. But I kept getting stuck on one particular point, and then someone pointed out that this wasn’t what we were supposed to talk about. I just felt like maybe my ideas weren’t as solid as expected. And so I decided to just sit down and start over again, making sure everything was perfect before I showed it to anyone else. It was hard, but I ended up missing the deadline anyway, which made me really feel bad about myself. My mind keeps telling me that if I don’t fix this flaw, I’ll never get noticed and people will find out how incompetent I am. So I’m just trying to figure out ways to cope better and stay positive going forward.

Yeah, I guess right after the dinner, I started feeling like I was always second guessing myself. Like maybe I’m not good enough, or maybe people don’t really care about me anymore. It’s hard to talk about stuff because I know it won’t make any difference, but I just feel so stuck and hopeless. I kept telling myself I wasn’t good enough until someone said, “No one ever really knows how great you really are.” But even then, it just made everything worse. I ended up being late again last night and apologized anyway, thinking it wouldn’t affect me, but now I feel so guilty and ashamed inside. I’ve tried to push out of the conversation, but it just makes things worse. Sometimes I just sit there and feel like I’m drowning in this endless sea of self-doubt and insecurity. It’s exhausting. I just want to go home and forget about it all, but it’s like I can’t stop thinking about it and I’m just stuck here.

So, last night we were invited to a casual gathering. I had no idea what to expect, so I decided to go. But when I arrived, everyone was talking about me like they didn’t care. I just sat there feeling really uncomfortable. And then I remembered some of the things I said back at work, and I’m scared I’ll sound weird or unappreciated. So I went home and stayed quiet, trying to ignore all the negativity. Now I feel really alone and sad, wondering if I did something wrong. It’s hard not to worry that I won’t impress anyone anymore.

Hey, did you hear about the dinner? Mom said everyone was talking about the kid’s brother getting promoted at work, but I didn’t get any recognition for my own accomplishments. I just sat there, trying to figure out why, and I can’t seem to shake this feeling of being unappreciated. It makes me feel so sad inside, like maybe I’m not good enough. And then later when I try to talk to my mom, she seems more upset than anything else—she says things like “everyone wants to know” and “you’re never going to impress anyone.” I feel like I’m just another toy in her grand plan. I’ve tried to hide it all night, but every time I open my mouth, it just comes back around again. I guess it’s just… too much for me to handle right now.

So, last night, I was really excited to come here because I’ve been really looking forward to joining this community group. But when I started talking there, all I could hear was just... “No one understands.” It made me feel like everyone else didn’t even care about me at all. I tried to hide my thoughts and try to fit in, but every time they’d say something, I felt like I was missing out on important conversations. I guess I just wanted to make sure everyone understood how I’m doing, but then I ended up feeling like maybe I wasn’t good enough. I guess I just stuck to my usual routine and tried to stay polite and confident, thinking maybe they wouldn’t notice. But it definitely made me feel like no one cares anymore. I feel so lost right now.

So, this time, there was just this casual chat going on where people weren’t really talking to each other. I guess I just didn’t feel included, even though I was trying to contribute something meaningful. Sometimes I wonder if maybe people aren’t paying attention because I’m not doing anything special or interesting. And then, after everything kind of goes downhill, I end up feeling pretty alone. Honestly, it makes me feel like nobody actually cares about me at all. Maybe I’m not good enough, or maybe I’m just being too shy around groups. I try not to dwell on these feelings, but sometimes I just can’t shake them off. I keep thinking, “Maybe if I did something different today, everyone might notice.” But it seems like it never happens, and I end up feeling pretty frustrated with myself. That’s why I usually stay back later, just sitting there thinking about how I felt last time.

So, this project means... well, I’m afraid it’s going to be another situation where they’re seeing I can’t handle it. They’re probably already doubting why I was chosen. And every time I try to talk to them about what happened, they just keep saying “I don’t understand.” It’s like they’ve figured out I’m not good enough, so there’s no point in trying anymore. I feel like I’m drowning in this, and I’m starting to doubt if I’m even worth being here at all. It’s scary and overwhelming. I just hope everything goes smoothly so I don’t get caught up in these endless discussions.

So, I got another bill notice, and I’m really worried because I didn’t even know there was one. It’s like this is going to happen again and again. I’m trying to ignore it, but it just seems to pile up faster than I can handle it. I feel so helpless, and I’m just so ashamed of how stupid I must look. I’ve been avoiding looking at the bill anymore, even if I need to open it. I keep thinking that maybe I’m just not good enough to manage finances, so I end up doing things that don’t really solve the problem. My hands get so shaky when I think about it, and it makes me feel even more lost and uncertain. I guess I just feel stuck in this endless cycle where nothing seems to work.

I’ve been having this weird feeling lately, like maybe there’s something wrong with our connection. At work, I was trying to make friends with someone, and then suddenly, they started hanging out with other people. It felt so awkward and uncomfortable, especially since I’m usually shy around them. I’m starting to worry that maybe I’m not good enough to connect with anyone anymore. I don’t want to go back to that meeting, but I can’t help but feel like everyone’s ignoring me right now. It’s hard to stay positive because I’m constantly worried that things won’t work out between us. My heart pounds, and I just feel like giving up. I hope this isn’t some big mistake, but I’m terrified that what happened tonight will never change anything.

Yeah, I’ve been feeling like I’m just a puppet controlled by everyone else. They’re all trying to make sure I don’t fail, even when I might just be able to help out. It’s exhausting, especially when I’m stuck on something simple. Sometimes I feel so scared of making a big decision that I stay quiet and don’t say anything at all. But later, when people start talking again, I feel even worse, like I’m drowning in this never-ending sea of pressure. It’s hard to face them and actually talk about what really matters to me anymore. It’s frustrating, but also deeply painful.

So, today I got some bad news from my bank statement. It says there was an extra payment amount. That means I’m facing more debt than expected, which makes it hard to pay it off. And I’ve been thinking about how people might look at me when they realize that I don’t actually own anything, especially after all these months of trying to hide things. I try not to mention it too much because I worry that maybe they’ll find out and then think less of me. I’m usually quiet around family and friends, so they won’t notice anything unless it’s really big. I guess I just feel trapped in this cycle of feeling like I’m always inadequate and alone. My mind keeps telling me that if I can’t handle my finances, no one will ever care enough to stay with me anymore. But I know that’s not true; everyone just wants me to succeed, even if it’s not easy. So, I’m just going to have to keep living my old life and pretending everything’s okay. It’s frustrating, but at least I haven’t lost any friends yet. I’ll just have to wait and see what happens next.

Yeah, lately, I’ve been having some tough days. They’re like, “You know what? I’m too good to care anymore. Or maybe they just don’t understand how much I matter.” It’s hard because I want to show them, but also keep myself safe. Sometimes I feel so desperate to just stay there and pretend everything’s okay, but then I start to doubt it all. It makes me feel so alone, so scared, and like no one understands me. I guess sometimes I’m just too afraid to face the truth, and I end up hiding behind these defenses to protect myself. I really need help figuring this out, but I’m so stuck. Can you give me some advice on how to navigate this?

Yeah, last week I had to take on two big projects at once. It seems like everyone thinks I’m just going to sit there and deal with these things because no one wants to hear about what I’ve got planned. So I worked until midnight, and when I finally did finish, I ended up missing another meeting, which made me feel like I didn’t even try. I kept trying to convince myself it wasn’t so bad, but deep down, I knew it was just a waste of time. But I couldn’t stop. I tried to push through, but it felt like I was drowning in a sea of paperwork and stress. It’s exhausting, and I don’t want to look foolish anymore. I guess I just need to give up.

So, last night I was assigned a big project at work. I had to collaborate with a bunch of other colleagues, and they were expecting some pretty tough stuff. I kept getting overwhelmed, especially after we started talking about it. Honestly, I don’t feel like I’m capable of handling this, and I’ve been avoiding any kind of discussion or even just putting off trying out because I know it won’t go well. Sometimes I just feel so stressed and anxious, like no matter what I do, it’ll just make things worse. It’s really exhausting and frustrating, and I don’t want to face anything more. I guess it’s just all stuck up in here.

So, last night, I received a notification from your office about some late bills and low balance. I’m worried because I’ve never had such problems before. But I keep putting off trying to open the mail or check my bank accounts. I’m so scared that if I don’t make these payments, it’ll all go away, and I won’t be able to explain anything. I keep thinking it’s not really happening, and if I do something silly like spend more than necessary, everyone will know I’m a total fool. It feels like there’s no end to what’s going on, and I just can’t get through to anyone about it. Sometimes I just want to throw myself on the couch and fall asleep, but then I wake up every day feeling even worse. I’m so afraid of disappointing others that I don’t dare ask for help or admit how much trouble I’m having. I guess I’m just too scared to face my fears, and I’m afraid I might never find a solution.

Yeah, I guess I got caught off guard when they gave us this big project to do. I started thinking, "I’m just not good enough for them, so I’ll never be able to make it." It’s like everyone thinks I’m just not smart enough to handle these things. I usually try to hide everything from people, but now I just feel really overwhelmed. Sometimes I just want to quit, but then I realize I don’t actually want to do it anyway. I feel so stressed out that even though I’ve put in lots of effort, I still can’t finish it. And I know that’s not very productive either, but I can’t shake this feeling that I’m just not cut out for it.

Yeah, it started off pretty normal. But then suddenly they stopped checking back, like every time I called or sent text, it felt like they were ignoring me completely. I started getting worried, like maybe they’re thinking I’m not good enough anymore. Then, after some time went by, I got really mad at myself for sending those messages, hoping that whatever happened was just a mistake. But I kept apologizing again and again, making it seem like I did something wrong, even though I knew I didn’t mean anything bad. And the longer I stayed there, the more desperate I got, wanting to feel better but knowing it wouldn’t help. It’s exhausting and makes me feel so helpless, like nobody cares about me anymore. I feel like I’m drowning in this thing, and I just want to go away but I know that’ll only make things worse. My mind keeps going through these endless loops, and I’m just stuck in a cycle of self-doubt and despair. It’s frustrating, and honestly, I don’t understand why they keep pushing me to do this all the time. I guess they’re just afraid I might be too stubborn or maybe I’m just not good enough for them anymore. But right now, I just want to scream, but I know I can’t. It’s a big burden, and I don’t want to deal with it anymore. It’s like they’ve found a weakness in me that no amount of talking can fix, and I’m just stuck in this never-ending loop of self-loathing and rejection. I feel trapped, and it’s killing me inside.

So, yesterday we were having dinner at the restaurant together. We were discussing our plans for the weekend, and I was really nervous about coming home empty-handed because everyone seemed so excited about the evening. But then… I decided to ask about the movie tonight. But as I started talking about it, my mind kept going back to how much I already wanted it, and then I couldn’t resist asking for something else that wasn’t there. After that, I didn’t even bother saying anything about it. It was just another day of procrastination, and I’m afraid this time it’ll happen again.

So, last night we had a family gathering at home where everyone was just so busy talking and focusing on other people. When I tried to join them earlier, I saw others were just sitting there, not paying any attention to me. I just kept thinking, "I’m not going to fit in here anymore." It makes me feel really lonely and afraid that maybe no one likes me back. I’ve been trying to stay away from it lately, but it just seems like it’s always happening again. I don’t want to talk to them, even though I know they’re probably just bored and don’t care much about what I have to say. I guess it’s just because I’m so used to feeling ignored and left out that I just can’t handle it anymore.

So, last night, some of our family members had a plan for this weekend. I guess they figured out how to make us wait until after midnight so they wouldn’t let us know we weren’t invited. I just sat there and felt so alone and rejected. I tried to stay silent, but then I started feeling really anxious and maybe even a little scared that maybe I’m not good enough. I’ve been avoiding these gatherings ever since, and lately, I’ve been having trouble talking to friends and family about it. It’s hard to believe that everyone else is okay and that I’m the only one left behind. I guess I’m just too afraid to face the truth and be seen as anything less than useless. It’s exhausting to feel like no one cares about me at all. I’ll go back home tonight and try again tomorrow, but right now, I just want to cry and hope that something better happens next time.

Yeah, this morning I was talking to some of my coworkers at work, and we were discussing our weekend plans. I just thought, “Well, maybe they’re going to think I’m not interesting enough.” But then I realized, they probably won’t invite me out because it seems like everyone else has plans. So instead, I ended up laughing and saying something silly about being a “homebody,” which made my coworkers laugh too. I didn’t really want to talk about it anymore, so I just kept going on with other things.

So, you were invited to speak at our team meeting today, and I was really nervous because everyone seems so interested in what I’ve done lately. But it went pretty well, and then they kept asking me some follow-up questions, which made me feel like maybe I wasn’t ready enough. So I had to leave early and stay quiet until they finished. I guess that just made me feel trapped, like nobody could see my true self anymore. I’m just so scared of embarrassing myself again. It’s really hard to talk about these things, especially when people are watching. And I don’t know why I always feel so anxious about making mistakes or disappointing others. It’s exhausting.

So, yesterday we were talking about our future plans. When I mentioned moving in together, I just couldn’t help nodding because I know it would mean losing them forever. But then, I felt like maybe they’d figure out something else and probably won’t want to be around anymore. So I kept quiet and tried not to bring up anything personal, but it just made things worse. I’m so scared of what people might think if I tell them I’m struggling or don’t want to be there anymore. It’s like they’re seeing me through the glass, thinking I’m too weak or unimportant. I feel really overwhelmed and lost.

So, today we were talking about the family dinner, and I was thinking about how I did really poorly at the job interview. And then everyone started talking about what happened there, and I felt like maybe my decision wasn’t even that great after all. So I kind of stayed quiet for a bit, didn’t say anything, and just avoided looking at anyone. After the meal, I kept thinking about it, and I’m trying to figure out why I’m so embarrassed about this. It feels like my whole body is telling me something bad is happening, and I don’t want to admit it anymore. I guess I’ll just try to ignore it and pretend nothing’s wrong.

So, lately, I’ve been having some tough times at work. I’m feeling stuck in my current position, even though I’ve tried everything to improve. I’m worried that if I don’t make some drastic changes, I’ll just be unable to solve these problems any longer. And then there’s this feeling of being too small, like nobody cares enough about me anymore. It makes me feel like there’s no point in trying, especially because I haven’t done anything wrong. I just want to feel better, but every time I try to talk to anyone, I end up feeling worse than before. It’s exhausting, and I just can’t shake the feeling that maybe I’m never going to get out of this hole. I’m just so afraid of losing my job or falling behind others, and it’s making everything feel hopeless. It’s really frustrating and overwhelming, and I don’t know what to do next. I just hope someone can help me figure this out, but I don’t know if I’m ready to face this challenge alone.

So, you were assigned a new project last night, and I’m just... feeling so overwhelmed. I’ve been working all day, and I keep thinking, “Maybe I’ll mess it up.” But I know I have to go ahead because it’s really important. I kept skipping breakfast and sleeping extra hours just to show them how important it is, hoping they wouldn’t notice. Honestly, I feel like I’m drowning in this thing, but I don’t want to let anyone down. So, I just said yes, even though it feels wrong. It’s exhausting, but I guess I have to keep pushing forward. Maybe I’ll get better tomorrow when I finally finish. It’s hard to stay positive right now, especially when everything seems so hopeless. Just… I’m scared.

So, let’s start with this recent dinner at the family gathering. As usual, I’m sitting there, trying to keep an eye on everyone around me. And then… oh no! They kept interrupting me, asking all sorts of questions and making me feel like nobody really cares about what I have to say. I stayed quiet until later, but even then, I felt like I had made them look down on me and that I wasn’t being heard. After that, I decided to leave early, but I ended up feeling very exhausted and overwhelmed. I just wanted to go home and maybe find somewhere else to sit alone and cry. But it’s hard to admit that I don’t want to stay here anymore, especially after everything that happened. So I probably won’t come back tonight.

So, last night, the family gathered to talk about something important and I was kind of left out. I felt like no matter what happened, nobody seemed to care about me, so I decided to stay away and try not to bother anyone else. I haven’t seen my family since then, and it’s been really hard to connect with them anymore. I feel really bad about myself, and I’m just so frustrated that no one seems to want to talk to me. It makes me feel like I’m never going to fit in anywhere, which can really make me feel lost and alone. I guess all these negative thoughts keep coming back to me, and I just can’t seem to shake off them. I know it might sound silly, but sometimes I wish I could just disappear forever and never come back.

So last night we had our family meeting about planning a trip together. I was trying to give some ideas, but they kept dismissing everything. I didn’t feel like I could add much value without being heard, so I stayed quiet and tried to leave early. But it just seemed like everyone just didn’t care, and I ended up feeling really stuck and defeated. I guess this makes me feel so guilty and like I’m not good enough for anyone else’s opinion. It’s exhausting to stay in that state, and I wonder why I keep having these same thoughts every time I go back to work. It’s hard to believe that no one thinks I’m capable unless I’m actually trying, and I know that’s not the case. I might as well just go back to sleep, but that’s probably better than staying here all day, right? So I’m just going to sit here and wait until someone comes around and asks me something important. Maybe tomorrow I’ll figure out how to make a difference. It’s frustrating, but I just can’t seem to shake off this feeling.

So, I was there last night at the party. We were talking about some personal stuff, and then suddenly someone starts talking about how everyone seems really connected and confident about their relationship. I just feel like I’m being left out and maybe I didn’t fit in. I tried to join in but ended up checking my phone, and I guess I said something stupid because they’re not interested in hearing what I had to say. I’ve been thinking lately that maybe I’m just too afraid to speak up and feel seen, especially since most people seem comfortable around me anyway. But I know I shouldn’t let this make me feel lonely anymore, so I’m trying to stay positive and not bother to talk about anything else tonight. Maybe tomorrow I’ll go somewhere quieter and feel better about myself.

So, last night we had our big presentation deadline coming up. Everyone was really looking forward to seeing how you did today. But I’m just getting stuck on some of the slides, especially those where they ask for feedback. Sometimes even when I try to explain things clearly, people seem to think I’m not good enough. It makes me feel like everyone thinks I’m just not cut out for this job. And honestly, I don’t want to go back to work because of all these doubts and worries. I guess I’ve just become too anxious lately.

So, yesterday, I got this notification and then I forgot to check my balance. I tried to go back later but when I checked my account today, I saw there was an overdraft. My mind keeps telling me I did something wrong, so I keep avoiding it and thinking maybe I’m just not good enough. It makes me feel really bad and helpless, especially because even though I’ve already told my mom and dad about it, they still seem surprised and disappointed. I don’t know what to do anymore, and I end up feeling overwhelmed and hopeless. It’s making me question my entire life, and I can’t figure out how to fix it. It’s like I’m constantly having to prove myself, and no matter what I try, it always fails. That’s why I’m afraid of facing things head-on, and I just stay inside my comfort zone, which only makes things worse. I’ll never be able to handle my finances properly, and that’s why I keep putting off paying them off. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending cycle of being unable to make any progress. It’s frustrating and exhausting, and I don’t want to face these problems anymore. I just want to disappear, but I know that won’t work. I need help, but I’m scared to ask for it, and I’m too embarrassed to confront anyone. I hope you can understand how deeply I’m hurting and how much I need help. Please don’t let me go through this alone. I love you, and I’m begging you to help me find some relief.

Yeah, during the discussion, I tried to explain the topic to the group, but then I felt like I didn’t get the chance to speak because everyone seemed too focused on how I’m going to sound better than they did. But when I finally got around to talking, I kept avoiding eye contact, which made me feel embarrassed. Later, I replayed everything out loud, thinking maybe if I really tried harder, I could at least come across as competent. It’s pretty scary to think that people are judging me based on whether I actually participated. Sometimes I wish I had just stayed quiet instead of trying to argue my point. I guess this makes me feel ashamed and scared of being judged again.

So, last night, the family gathered to plan our next holiday together, but I was left out because we didn’t discuss it. I felt so ignored and lonely, especially since everyone seems to already know what I’m thinking and doing. I kept going back to my room and trying to hide from the others, but then I just ended up feeling very alone and sad. After that, I decided to stay home and wait until tomorrow, but I felt even worse after that. Now, I don’t know what to do anymore. My mind keeps replaying these same thoughts over and over again, and I feel like I’m constantly falling apart. It’s like everyone is judging me based on how I act and talk, and I’m just stuck in this endless cycle of rejection and loneliness. I guess all these feelings just get stronger and harder to deal with.

So, yesterday I was sitting there at dinner with my family and realized we weren’t even invited. I’ve been trying to tell myself I’m just not wanted, but every time I look back, I feel like I’m being left out. It makes me feel so lonely, and I keep thinking maybe I’m just not good enough or attractive enough to fit in. But deep down inside, I know I’m just not meant to belong here. It’s like this whole gathering is telling me I’m not valuable enough to be part of anything meaningful. So, I decided to stay home and watch TV instead, hoping that’ll bring some peace and quiet. But tonight, when I finally got home, I just couldn’t bear to go in. And then I woke up late and went straight to bed, where I stayed until the next day, thinking maybe tomorrow I’ll wake up and find out what happened. I’m feeling so lost and scared right now, and it’s making me feel really bad.

Yeah, during dinner today, I felt like I was always the only person talking, even when everyone else was there. It made me feel really alone. Then, I tried to hide behind my chair and talk less, but it just seemed too much effort to stay quiet. I ended up just staring at the TV, pretending to listen to everything but actually didn’t care what anyone said. That’s definitely making me feel worse than usual. I guess this makes me feel vulnerable and scared that no one cares about me anymore.

So, this week I’ve been assigned a big project at work. Everyone was dismissive of our ideas, so I’m feeling like nobody really cares about my input. I keep staying late because I don’t want to disappoint anyone. And when I say something isn’t working out, I just pretend to feel better, but it’s just another day where no one notices. It’s exhausting, and I end up skipping breakfast and having too many late nights. Sometimes I even joke around about how tough I must be to get noticed, but then I’m still trying to push through despite knowing deep down I’m failing. It’s making me feel so anxious and lonely right now.

Yeah, I guess I just kept thinking I’d never get it right. Sometimes I feel like I’m constantly falling behind because I don’t really know how to approach these problems. And I keep putting off trying out for that big presentation because I worry it’ll come back and show everyone how bad I am. It’s so scary to look at all the other people doing great projects and then having to take on something so different and challenging myself. My coworkers always ask what I’m doing next, and I feel embarrassed when I tell them I’m stuck. It’s exhausting to see everyone else getting through it, but I just want to stay safe and secure.

Hey [Therapist’s Name], I’ve been trying to decide if I should apply for this new job. It seems like everyone is talking about how scared I am and how I’m not good enough. But lately, I keep getting so anxious and afraid about what might happen if I don’t apply. I spend hours looking at resumes and trying to convince myself that maybe there’s something wrong with me. It’s exhausting and makes me feel even worse about myself. What do you think? Should I give it another shot?

Hey, so yesterday we had a networking event where everyone was trying to talk to each other. I just saw some of my old friends and started thinking, “Oh no, what did I miss out on? Maybe they’re all too shy or afraid to speak up.” And then I kept checking my phone to make sure they didn’t notice anything silly or weird happening between us, like if they seemed bored or unenthusiastic about the topic. I also tried to hide behind my smile and cover up any obvious mistakes I made. It’s really making me feel nervous and insecure. Sometimes I wish I could just sit back and wait until someone finally understands me, but I know deep down I’m still not good enough. So, I guess these thoughts are just getting harder to bear.

So, let’s talk about what happened yesterday. Yeah, last week, I got an offer for a weekend art class. But then, there were some tricky things happening at work – I’m supposed to finish my project early, but I’m also working on another important meeting that day. So, I decided to skip out on the art class, thinking maybe I’ll just wait until next week. But here comes the worst part, I feel so frustrated right now. I’m worried that if I don’t attend, I might miss out on all the fun and growth opportunities that come with it. And honestly, I just want to know why this isn’t possible, especially since it seems like everyone else had such easier times. I keep telling myself it’s just too much pressure, but I can’t shake the feeling that no matter how hard I try, I just never really make it. So, I’m just choosing not to go, hoping it will fix everything.

So, today we had another dinner where my parents were really pushing me towards something that I didn’t really feel like I wanted to do. It felt pretty tight in there, like everyone was just trying to get me to conform to these old ways of thinking. I just stayed quiet, and after dinner, I just sat alone in my room and kept thinking, "If I ever did anything different, then maybe I’d actually be able to escape this whole family thing." It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I’m okay and capable of doing things on my own, but my parents won’t let me explore any other paths. So, I just sit here and feel like there’s nothing I can do right now, and I’m starting to doubt if I’ll ever truly find happiness outside of this family system. I feel so stuck, and it’s hard to even talk to anyone about it anymore. I guess I just need to keep going back to my comfort zone and not look too far away. But it’s also scary to say goodbye to people who care so much about me and believe in me. I’m scared that if I start exploring new things, they might change their minds about how important I am or how comfortable I am being around them. I just feel like I’m stuck in this cycle of feeling like I’m always wrong and nobody likes me anymore. It’s exhausting, and I’m definitely struggling to cope with this situation.

Yeah, it happened again at work. I kept trying to explain everything, but some of my points seemed to get lost in the crowd. I tried to keep up with what everyone else was saying, and it felt like I was drowning in their comments. Then, after I finished, I went straight to my desk and sat there, thinking maybe I was just not good enough. I ended up sending an email explaining all the things I didn’t cover, feeling embarrassed and ashamed. It made me feel so guilty that I can’t even face anyone anymore. I’m scared to go back to the meetings because I worry they’ll realize I’m not as competent as I think. I guess this whole thing is just weighing too heavily on me right now. I don’t know if I can handle this anymore.

Yeah, lately I’ve been trying to get some projects done, but it keeps getting pushed back. Sometimes I feel like maybe I’m just not good enough, or I’m too hard on myself. It’s frustrating because then I might not get noticed anymore. I keep asking people how they’re doing when I’m supposed to be working, and they always say something along those lines like “They must think I’m so incompetent.” It’s exhausting and makes me feel trapped, like no matter what I do, there’s always going to be someone else looking out for them. I’ve been putting in extra hours to prove myself, but I end up spending less time with friends and family, and it’s starting to affect my mood and overall well-being. My coworkers always try to help me, but I’m just not cut out for teamwork or pushing boundaries. I guess all these things just seem to happen to me, and I don’t know what to do about it.

So, last night we had this meeting where we were supposed to finish all these projects really fast, but I kept getting distracted and worried about forgetting something important. I tried to focus on my work and even skipped dinner because I feared I wouldn’t make it. But every time I did feel like I wasn’t doing well enough, and I just ended up feeling so guilty and ashamed. So I’ve been trying to work very late, skip meals, and spend more time alone. I’m starting to get pretty drained and stressed out too, and I just feel like maybe if I can just figure out how to get through this quickly next time, I won’t look like such a fool. It’s kind of a never-ending battle, and I’m just stuck here. I guess I just want to know if anyone here actually cares about me or if they’re just going to tell everyone I’m not good enough.

Yeah, I’ve been noticing they’re all focused on me, like how they just don’t seem interested in what I’m saying. It’s like everyone else is just too busy being better than me to notice. I feel so alone here, and I guess I’m just not good enough anymore. Sometimes I just want to stay out of it, but then I start thinking maybe I’m not really wanted anywhere. It’s hard to talk to anyone about it, especially since we usually keep talking about other things. Maybe if I did something different next time, people wouldn’t say anything about me. But right now, everything feels like it’s going nowhere.

Yeah, I guess I felt like maybe I wasn’t good enough or important enough in our conversations. It’s weird because I’ve always felt like someone was trying too hard to connect with me. But then again, I didn’t really want to talk about things anymore, so maybe it was just another day of being missed. I just sat there all night, replaying how they were supposed to listen and what I said back, and ended up feeling even worse than when I started. I’m just so stuck here, trying to figure out why everything just keeps going wrong. Maybe it’s time to let go and focus on something else entirely.

So, last night we decided to gather around the table to share some updates about our day at work. When I tried to participate, there was no response from anyone else, so I stayed silent. I didn’t even say anything before leaving the room. I’m sure they were all busy talking about their own lives, and I didn’t want to bother them anymore. I guess I just felt like nobody cared enough about me, especially since everyone seems to know exactly what I’m thinking right now.

So, yesterday we went out for lunch and when I tried to join in, everyone seemed to be focused on their own stuff. It was awkward because I didn’t really want to talk anymore, so I just sat there, trying to stay quiet. And after dinner, I stayed inside all night, thinking about this whole thing. My mind kept wandering back to the fact that no one seemed interested in talking to me. It’s making me feel really sad and rejected. I guess sometimes I just can’t stand it anymore.

So, you were invited to join a workshop next month, right? I was thinking maybe I could drop out because I’m worried I’ll mess things up or people will find out. But then I kept thinking, “Maybe I’ll just let it all slide and pretend nothing happened.” Or maybe I’ll just sit there and wait until someone else does better than I did last time. And honestly, I don’t want to talk about it anymore because it feels so scary. So, I decided to skip it and just stay home, trying to figure out what went wrong. It’s frustrating though – I really didn’t mean to miss it, but it’s like I’m always doing something wrong anyway. Maybe it’s just too much work coming up or maybe I just need to learn how to handle stress better. But I don’t want to admit these feelings to anyone, especially not my friends or family. It’s okay to ask for help sometimes, but I just don’t want to take the chance of making things worse. I guess that’s why I’m avoiding it, even though it seems obvious. So, thank you for asking, I appreciate you understanding me better.

Yeah, this was tough. I tried to stay calm but… I ended up agreeing anyway, even though I didn’t really care. Then, I just kept thinking about how people might find out if I’m being honest, which made me feel bad about not speaking up. Honestly, it’s embarrassing to admit things when no one wants to hear from me. But maybe that’s normal, especially when you’re afraid of rejection? It’s frustrating because I wanted to share my ideas but feared they wouldn’t come across well. Maybe it was just a big mistake on my part. But then again, maybe it was just my ego getting hurt, and I didn’t realize it until later. I guess I just needed to speak up, but I always knew better than to say anything important. So yeah, it was pretty messed up.

So, last night, I was texting them again and they didn’t reply at all. I’m like, “Okay, I can’t keep this up,” and then I just feel really stressed. Sometimes I wonder if maybe they’ve gotten bored or disappointed with me because I haven’t done enough. And even though I try to reassure them, it’s just making things worse. I’ve been trying to hide my true feelings, but I just end up feeling like I’m betraying myself. It’s like I’m constantly checking on whether they’re okay, which makes me feel guilty. And sometimes I’ll ask my friends for help, wondering if they understand what I’m going through. But they usually say something along the lines of, "Oh, I guess they might just like it anyway," and that just makes everything worse. My mind keeps racing back to where I left off, and I get so frustrated I just can’t stop worrying. I’m definitely not doing very well at this relationship.

So, last night I had an awkward meeting with some friends. I didn’t even know anyone there until after we were done talking. I kept thinking that everyone else probably didn’t want to hang out because they’re too busy with other people. It made me feel really bad about myself and kind of depressed. So I decided to stay home and just sit alone, trying to figure out what happened. But instead of talking to them, I ended up spending the whole weekend feeling so confused and like I’m not part of any group anymore. I guess this just makes me feel worse than usual.

Yeah, last night I got a notice for overdue utility bills. I’ve already paid my rent, but I’m still having trouble paying them this month. I feel like I’m somehow responsible for everything, and everyone seems to be handling their own finances better than me. It makes me feel even more ashamed. And I’m really avoiding talking about it with anyone, even if I know they’re probably too scared to hear me. Sometimes I just feel trapped, like no matter how hard I try, I can’t fix this. I guess I just don’t believe in myself anymore.

Last week, I received a notification about overdue bills and an upcoming rent increase. I was overwhelmed, anxious, and felt like I could never handle this. I started avoiding conversations about finances because I fear others will judge me if I talk about it. I’ve been staying home and avoiding social events to keep myself safe. It’s hard to face the reality of being financially struggling, especially since I’m used to hiding behind excuses. I feel like everyone thinks I’m not capable, so maybe I just don’t deserve any more support. My emotions are intense, and I find myself struggling to cope with these situations. I’m scared people will notice how helpless I am, and I worry about losing face or getting left out. It feels like a constant reminder that I’m not good enough, and I can’t change anything about myself. I really need to talk to someone who understands what I’m going through, but I’m afraid of hurting anyone or making them feel worse. I hope they can understand how overwhelming this feels and offer comfort and support instead of just dismissing my feelings. Please help me navigate this situation and remind me that I’m not alone.

So, today I was assigned a big project with many different teams. I’ve been trying to stay organized so I don’t get distracted, but I keep getting these nagging feelings that I’m just too afraid to tackle anything because people think I’m not smart enough. Sometimes I even ask for help, but I end up feeling like no one understands what I’m doing. I usually tell them off or say I’m too scared, but then they either dismiss me or make excuses. It makes me feel like I’m being made fun of or seen as unimportant, and that’s pretty crushing. I tried to push myself harder, but it just seems to go against my own beliefs. It’s making me feel very low and like nobody wants to work with me anymore. My mind keeps telling me things like, "Don’t worry, you’ll never fit in here," but it’s really hurting me. I just hope everything gets better soon.

So, today we had this big meeting. Everyone was talking excitedly about what they’ve done recently. But as we talked, I kept thinking, "I’m just not good enough. Everyone else is doing better than me, and I’m not even close. I feel like I’m just another cog in the machine, nobody cares about me." It’s like I’m always being left behind, feeling like I’m just a small part of something bigger. So after the meeting, I stayed quiet for a few hours and then decided to go away and deal with everything. Back at work, I spent the whole day just thinking about it and wondering if anyone noticed or cared about how I felt. I guess that’s why I didn’t talk much today.

So, today I got a notification about some overdrawn account and bills. It’s like I’ve messed up something big. But hey, everyone else seems okay, so what if I mess up? I don’t want them to see how bad I’m actually doing things. So, I’ll just keep quiet and ignore it. Maybe later when I feel really down, I’ll ask someone if there’s anything wrong with my finances. That way, I won’t feel completely useless anymore. But right now, I’m just trying to stay calm and not let them know I’m having a tough day. I’ll probably just sit here all night watching TV until they call me back anyway. It’s exhausting.

Yeah, last night I got a notification about some unexpected fees on my bank account. I’m feeling really overwhelmed right now because I know these aren’t just small bills—they’re huge and I don’t want to take them personally. I’ve been trying to keep things under wraps, but I feel like everyone is watching me. I’m definitely going to have to ask for help next time so I can make sure I haven’t messed anything up. It’s like this is just another way for me to stay hidden and get away from what I really need. I’m so scared of being seen as incompetent and rejected.

So, recently, I’ve been having some trouble adjusting to our new relationship. It’s almost like everyone thinks I’m failing out of touch because when we talk, there’s this sense that maybe they don’t really care about what I have to say. I keep thinking, though, that if I just stick around and let them see how much I want to stay, then they’ll eventually move on. But it’s making me feel so scared. Sometimes I just want to give up and go back home, but at other times, I can’t help feeling trapped in my own little world. It’s exhausting, and I’m starting to wonder if I’m ever going to make it through this. So, I guess all I can do is hope that maybe things will work out better soon.

So, during our last team meeting, I was asked to share my opinion on this project. Usually, I just nod and smile. But today, I’m going to go ahead anyway, thinking maybe they’ll like it anyway. I guess I can’t really explain too much since they’re already saying things that seem right to me. I’ll just keep trying to convince them, even if it adds some extra pressure. It feels so comfortable, knowing I’m helping them feel better. But deep down, I know it won’t help unless I actually make something meaningful. And if I fail, everyone might think I’m not good enough. That’s what makes me anxious. I’m scared they’ll find out I’m not as great as everyone thinks. It’s frustrating. I need someone to understand how I feel, but right now, I just want to pretend everything’s okay. Can you believe this? It’s almost like they’re counting on me being the one to screw up and embarrass me. I just hope they see through my lies and realize there’s nothing wrong with me. Maybe it’s just a coincidence, or maybe they just don’t trust me. I’m afraid of rejection and all the other stuff that comes with it, and I don’t know why I’m doing this anymore. I’m just hoping it’s not too late. Could we talk about something else soon? I’d love to hear your thoughts on this project.

So, you were assigned a big project right after lunch today. I’m so nervous! I keep thinking, “If I fail, then everyone will see I’m really bad. Maybe they’ll give me another chance.” But deep down, I feel like I’m failing because I haven’t practiced enough or tried too hard. I’ve skipped some meetings and stayed up all night, afraid people might notice my mistakes. It’s frustrating, but I know I have to push through. Sometimes I just want to throw it away and never come back. But it’s just making me feel even more stuck and defeated. I guess it’s just a cycle where I keep putting off things until they’re too late. I don’t know what to do anymore. Can we talk about something else?

Yeah, so lately, we’ve been having some awkward conversations at work. It feels like no matter how many times we talk, there’s still something missing from our discussion. Sometimes it’s just not clear whether they really understand what I’m trying to say or if they’re just going through the motions. I feel like I’m always being judged, which makes me feel very ashamed and worthless. But then I try to hide them away, hoping it’ll go away. After all, maybe they’re just too busy to notice. So, I ended up leaving earlier and didn’t bother to come back again, thinking maybe they won’t even remember. Honestly, it’s pretty exhausting sometimes. I guess I just need to keep pushing forward and hope that someday they’ll realize that they missed out on something important.

Hey, so this morning when I got the notice about the bills and the bank account getting too high, I just felt really bad. It’s like everyone thinks I’m just failing because it seems so obvious that I’m never going to make it through this. My mind keeps replaying the idea that everyone must think I’m a failure and that they’re all just trying to get rid of me. I’ve tried to talk to some friends, but they don’t seem interested in what’s happening. I feel like I’m drowning in these feelings, and I’m scared of letting anyone know about them. So I decided to stay home and avoid talking about it, hoping nobody notices. But instead of calming down, I just ended up isolating myself even more. It’s just frustrating and overwhelming, and I don’t want to go through this anymore.

Last night, our partner commented on how little we talk lately. I guess he thinks I’m being too needy, or maybe I’m just not good enough for him anymore. So, I’ve started spending more time alone at home, even though he doesn’t ask me out again. It feels like he wants me around more because I’m not there to fill his empty spots. But then again, I’m worried that if I don’t get back into the game soon, he might decide to go away forever. I keep trying to remind myself that everything is okay, but every time I push harder, it just makes things worse. It’s exhausting, and I’m struggling to cope with the stress.

So recently, I had been assigned a big project that required a lot of collaboration and innovative thinking. But lately, whenever I think about it, I start second-guessing everything. It feels like there’s always some kind of flaw lurking somewhere, and it makes me feel even more isolated and trapped. Sometimes I just sit there, staring at the screen and replaying all the ways I’ve failed—people look away, colleagues laugh at me, and I’m left feeling helpless and defeated. I’ve tried to push out any negative thoughts, but they just keep creeping back in. And I know deep down, I’m just not cut out for this. It’s like I’m constantly putting pressure on myself to fail, but I never really get a chance to succeed because of all the things I’ve overlooked. So I’m just... waiting for something to happen. Maybe next time someone sees what I did, it’ll make me realize how stupid I must have been. It’s exhausting trying to pull myself away from this thing, but sometimes it feels like it’s the only way out.

So, last night, I got a notice about some recent payments being due. And it seems like something went wrong when I tried to pay them. I’ve been trying to figure out what happened, but all I’m doing is spending hours on this. It feels like I’m constantly putting things off and getting stuck, even though I know there must be some way out. Sometimes I just don’t want to talk about it anymore. I guess I’m afraid people might think I’m failing because I’m always finding excuses to not deal with these things. It’s making me feel really bad and helpless. Maybe it’s just me being stubborn, but I keep thinking maybe I’m just not good enough. I guess I need to start living my life differently, but every time I try, I end up back where I started. I’ll probably just keep ignoring these reminders and pretending everything’s okay until someone comes along and tells me it’s nothing. That’s it. I just can’t bear dealing with these problems anymore.

I’m sorry, I’ve been avoiding these discussions at this family gathering. I feel so lost and alone here. Sometimes I wonder if anyone cares enough about me to notice what I’m doing or say anything meaningful. It makes me feel like I’m just an empty shell, and I’m always trying to prove myself wrong, but no one ever listens or sees any evidence. My anxiety peaks when I talk about how I don’t belong in this group, and I end up feeling like a complete failure rather than a valued member. It’s exhausting and leaves me feeling drained. I really need to talk to someone about this, but I’m afraid they’ll judge me or tell me I’m not good enough. It’s hard to keep going, especially when I’m already tired from all the work I’ve done lately. I hope you can understand how much this affects me. Please let me know if there’s anything I can do to change this.

So, yesterday we had this big project coming up. It was supposed to involve a lot of hard work and dedication, and I was really worried because everyone was looking at me and saying, "They’re not good enough," and I kept putting off doing anything. I started working extra late every night and couldn’t find time to eat or rest. But then I got so tired that I barely slept anymore. I’m still trying to make ends meet and keep going despite all these problems, and I just feel like I’m drowning in this endless cycle of failure. I’m scared that if I don’t manage to finish this project, people will think I’m not good enough or that I’m just not cut out for this kind of work. I guess I just want to believe that maybe I’m not cut out for this, but sometimes I wonder if I’m actually capable of doing this kind of work. I hope you can understand how much it’s affecting me.

So, you were assigned an extra project recently. But now, you’re feeling really stressed out because there’s just too much to do. You keep pushing yourself harder than usual and feel like no matter what you do, people won’t understand how hard it is for you. It’s making you feel like you’re walking around in a never-ending limbo, where nobody else seems to care about how you’re struggling. It’s exhausting to stay awake late at night, ignoring emails and calls from friends, and even skipping meals to focus on the task ahead. I’m scared you’ll realize how incompetent you are and maybe someone will think you’re a total failure. It’s like everyone thinks I’m a complete failure because of all these little things I’ve done wrong. I don’t know how to fix this, and it’s starting to make me feel lost and alone.

So, yesterday we had our team meeting, and this time I was supposed to contribute to a project that wasn’t very interesting to anyone. I guess when they saw my contribution, they didn’t see anything special there. So I decided to try harder and volunteer for more projects because if they notice me doing something important, maybe they’ll recognize me better. But then I ended up spending all day at work trying to impress them, and it made me feel like nobody cared about me anymore. I’m so sad right now, I don’t want to go home. It’s hard to talk to friends because I know I won’t get a chance to share my struggles anytime soon.

So, I decided to join the team because it seemed like we’re all having similar conversations lately. But when I saw the other members talking, I just felt like there wasn’t much to contribute. I kept trying to fit in and make eye contact, but I never really managed to grab anyone’s attention. Honestly, I didn’t even want to talk anymore at the end of the meeting. I guess it’s just me being too shy and not knowing how to connect with people. I’ll admit, I’m kind of nervous around groups, so maybe it’s just me missing out on opportunities to shine. But right now, I just feel like a little bit of embarrassment creeping up on me. I’ll just have to deal with it, okay? It’s hard to go home and tell everyone I’m not enjoying myself tonight.

So, lately, things got really tough at work. It's just so frustrating when all the good opportunities come back to haunt us. I keep thinking it might happen again, no matter how many times I try. I've tried talking to people about it, but they just don’t understand or care enough to listen. And then I start to feel even worse, like I’m losing everything because there’s nowhere else to go. It’s exhausting, and I just want to run away somewhere safe. But right now, I’m stuck here, and I don’t know what to do next. I’ve been trying to make better plans around this issue, but it keeps getting in my way. Sometimes I just wish I could just forget about it entirely, but it just won’t stop bothering me anymore. I’m scared of failure and rejection, and I just feel like it’s going to end up ruining everything. It’s like my life is spiraling out of control, and I’m just stuck in a never-ending cycle of disappointment and frustration. My anxiety is palpable, and I just feel trapped in a loop where I can’t escape. I need help, but I’m afraid to ask. It’s making me feel so helpless, and I don’t know if I can handle this anymore.

So, yesterday we had our big family gathering and I was sitting there alone, watching everyone else chat. I feel like someone never seems to ask about me anymore, so I’m kind of lost and confused. I tried talking to some friends, but they didn’t seem interested either. I guess it’s because they’re afraid to talk about me or because I’ve always been so quiet in these kinds of situations. I feel so bad for ignoring them, but maybe they didn’t even realize how much I missed them. I’ll probably stay home today until they come back, and then I won’t see anyone again. It’s frustrating and I feel like I’m drowning in this loneliness. I just hope everything goes better soon.

Yeah, I guess right after we started chatting outside, everyone seemed really comfortable around me, talking about fun things. So, I kept interrupting the conversation because I wanted to share what I felt, especially since I’m shy and nervous around strangers. But then, I’d ask if they liked me enough and feel like I was missing out. After the event, I ended up feeling pretty lonely, and I didn’t want to bring up any problems or make sure everyone enjoyed the night. It’s definitely making me anxious lately, especially when I’m around other people.

Yeah, so today we had our last team meeting where everyone was sitting there like, “Hey, look what happened! No one wanted to talk!” It’s kind of scary because I’m usually the one who gets dragged along and finds out what they’re doing all night anyway. So, I always leave earlier than usual. And sometimes I just sit there and think, “Well, maybe I’m just not good enough.” That makes me feel even worse because it’s like no matter how much I try, it never really works out. I’ve tried everything lately—maybe I’m just too quiet, or maybe I’m just too shy—but nothing seems to make a difference. It’s frustrating, especially when other people don’t seem to notice anything wrong either. So, I keep trying to hide behind my desk, hoping they’ll see something better. But it just feels like I’m being watched all the time, and I end up feeling even more lonely and alone than before.

Yeah, so yesterday at dinner, they kept saying I wasn’t talking much and then I tried to explain something but they just kept dismissing me like it didn’t matter. I feel really bad about this. I’m going to stay home and maybe talk to some friends later instead of joining them too. I guess that’s what makes me feel so bad—like everyone thinks I’m just not interested in anything anymore. It’s hard to keep going through all this stuff, especially when I know I’m not doing very well. I’ll probably go back to sleep tonight, thinking I’m nothing special anyway.

So, recently assigned a high-stakes project at work. I was so nervous, I almost froze right there. But then, I just couldn’t shake the feeling that something was wrong, even though I’ve always been pretty competent. I tried to push myself harder, but every time I take a step forward, I just feel like I’m failing again. It’s scary to admit that maybe I’m not good enough for what I’m doing, and people might think I’m not worth it anymore. My anxiety levels are soaring, and I find myself constantly checking emails and social media posts to see if anyone notices anything wrong. But deep down, I know that if I don’t finish this project, I’ll be seen as a complete failure, and no one will believe in me anymore. It’s just too much stress. I’m scared to ask for help, because if I do, it might come back to bite me later, and I won’t be able to fix it. It’s like I’m drowning in these feelings, and I’m having a hard time getting out of them. I guess I need to keep pushing through, but it’s hard to stay positive when all I’m thinking about is how bad things really are. I’m sorry if I’m causing any more problems, but I promise I’ll be better soon. Can we talk about something less stressful?

So, yesterday I started working on this big project. I felt really stressed out.
